text.title: GitLab CI/CD Security and Deployment
text.summary: Built-in security scan templates, protected variables, environments with review apps, and Docker/Kubernetes deploy patterns.

rule.statement: Include GitLab security templates via include: rather than writing custom SAST/DAST jobs.
rule.rationale: GitLab-maintained templates stay updated with new vulnerability signatures. Custom jobs require ongoing maintenance.

example.code:
include: - template: Security/SAST.gitlab-ci.yml - template: Security/Dependency-Scanning.gitlab-ci.yml - template: Security/Container-Scanning.gitlab-ci.yml - template: Security/Secret-Detection.gitlab-ci.yml
container_scanning: variables: CS_IMAGE: $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA
example.note: Override specific variables to point container scanning at your built image rather than the default.

rule.statement: Mark all CI/CD secrets as Protected AND Masked in GitLab variable settings.
rule.rationale: Protected limits the variable to protected branches/tags. Masked prevents the value from appearing in job logs. Both are required; neither alone is sufficient.

rule.statement: Define environment: name and url on every deploy job, and pair with an on_stop job.
rule.rationale: Environments enable deployment tracking, approval workflows, and rollback. Without on_stop, review namespaces accumulate and consume cluster resources.

example.code:
deploy-staging: stage: deploy environment: name: staging url: https://staging.example.com on_stop: stop-staging rules: - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
review-app: stage: deploy environment: name: review/$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID url: https://$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID.review.example.com on_stop: stop-review auto_stop_in: 1 week rules: - if: $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID
stop-review: stage: deploy variables: GIT_STRATEGY: none environment: name: review/$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID action: stop script: - kubectl delete namespace review-$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID --ignore-not-found when: manual rules: - if: $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID

rule.statement: Enable Docker BuildKit and use --cache-from with the latest tag to speed up image builds.
rule.rationale: Without layer cache reuse, every build starts from scratch. BuildKit parallelizes layer builds and enables cache import/export.

example.code: docker-build: stage: build image: docker:26 services: - docker:26-dind variables: DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR: "/certs" DOCKER_BUILDKIT: "1" IMAGE_TAG: $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA IMAGE_LATEST: $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest before_script: - docker login -u $CI_REGISTRY_USER -p $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD $CI_REGISTRY script: - docker build --cache-from $IMAGE_LATEST --build-arg BUILDKIT_INLINE_CACHE=1 --tag $IMAGE_TAG --tag $IMAGE_LATEST . - docker push $IMAGE_TAG - docker push $IMAGE_LATEST

rule.statement: Use when: manual for production deployments; use automated sync for staging.
rule.rationale: Production requires human gate. Staging should be continuously deployed to catch integration issues early.
